title: Desmocladus asper
taxonomy: Desmocladus asper is a species of plant in the family Restionaceae. It is native to Australia. The classification of Desmocladus asper includes the kingdom Plantae, phylum Liliophyta, and class Liliopsida. Further classification includes the order Poales, family Restionaceae, genus Desmocladus, and species D. asper. This species is often found in wetland environments.

intro: Desmocladus asper, also known as the rough tree fern, is a plant species native to Australia that belongs to the family Cyatheaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Desmocladus asper include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
